fact_nuggets.text: Aerilate (Japanese: Sky Skin) is an Ability introduced in Generation VI.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD Normal-type moves that the Pokemon uses become Flying type and deal more damage! MD RT DX Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon become Flying type and deal more damage.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation VI 1.1.2 Generation VII 1.1.3 Affected moves 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Aerilate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Flying-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Aerilate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Aerilate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Flying-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Aerilate, as well as the Pokemon that can have Aerilate and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Power and Boost Learned by Bind Normal Physical 15 18 Body Slam Normal Physical 85 102 Cut Normal Physical 50 60 Double Hit Normal Physical 35 42 Double-Edge Normal Physical 120 144 Facade Normal Physical 70 84 False Swipe Normal Physical 40 48 Feint Normal Physical 30 36 Flail Normal Physical Frustration Normal Physical Fury Attack Normal Physical 15 18 Giga Impact Normal Physical 150 180 Guillotine Normal Physical Headbutt Normal Physical 70 84 Hyper Beam Normal Special 150 180 Hyper Voice Normal Special 90 108 Quick Attack Normal Physical 40 48 Rage Normal Physical 20 24 Return Normal Physical Round Normal Special 60 72 Secret Power Normal Physical 70 84 Slash Normal Physical 70 84 Snore Normal Special 50 60 Strength Normal Physical 80 96 Swift Normal Special 60 72 Take Down Normal Physical 90 108 Thrash Normal Physical 120 144 Vise Grip Normal Physical 55 66 Although the following moves are affected by the Ability, they currently cannot be learned by any Pokemon with Aerilate. They may, however, be called via Metronome , Mirror Move , Copycat , or Mimic , or be used by a Pokemon that gained Aerilate by an Ability like Trace or Receiver or a move such as Skill Swap or Role Play . Special Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Aerilate has no effect outside of battle.

Contents 1 In battle 1.1 Generation VI 1.2 Generation VII 1.3 Affected moves In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Aerilate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Flying-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Aerilate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Aerilate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Flying-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Special
Turns the bearer's normal moves into flying moves and strengthens them to 1.3 their power.
